# Part 12 — Problem Patterns {.sdaia-dark background-gradient="linear-gradient(135deg, #1C355E, #00C9A7)"}

## What can a moving robot *actually do*?

A remote-controlled car is incredibly fun — but **useful robots solve real problems on their own**.

::: {.incremental}
- 💊 Deliver medicine inside a hospital
- 🔍 Inspect dangerous pipes underground
- 📦 Move boxes in a warehouse
- 🚜 Navigate a farm autonomously
:::

::: {.fragment}
**To do this safely, they need more than a human holding a remote control!**
:::

## The Pattern Behind Every Robot {.sdaia-dark background-gradient="radial-gradient(circle at 30% 70%, #0f172a 0%, #1C355E 100%)"}

Every robot program follows the same 3-step loop:

:::::: {.columns}

::::: {.column width="33%"}
### 1️⃣ Sense
Read the world — buttons, sensors, signals.
:::::

::::: {.column width="33%"}
### 2️⃣ Decide
Run logic — `if`, `switch`, functions.
:::::

::::: {.column width="33%"}
### 3️⃣ Act
Drive motors, blink LEDs, make sounds.
:::::

::::::

::: {.fragment}
Today you completed **Act** (Motors) and basic **Sense** (IR Receiver).
:::

## 💡 Curiosity Challenge

::: {.callout-tip}
## Try This at Home (totally optional, totally fun!)

**Can you improve your remote control?**

- What happens if you press a button on a regular TV remote pointing at the car?
- Can you change the speed of the motors so "Up" is fast and "Down" is slow?
- Can you add code to make the car automatically brake if you hold "OK"?

*Experiment, break things, and bring your findings next session!*
:::

## What's Coming Next?

::: {.incremental}
- **Session 3:** The Smarter Car — **Sensors** 🧠
- Give your car "eyes" using Ultrasonic Sound (like a bat!).
- Make the car drive itself and dodge obstacles automatically.
- Start brainstorming ideas for the WRO Future Innovators project!
:::

::: {.fragment}
### See you next time! 🚗💨
:::
